nodes.title: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phr...
nodes.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
nodes.subdomain: Language Standards

academic_standards.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

nodes.title: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and...
nodes.description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence, conveying a clear and distinct perspective, such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning, alternative or opposing perspectives are addressed, and the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and a range of formal and informal tasks.

nodes.title: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse formats and me...

nodes.title: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sou...
nodes.description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the strengths and limitations of each source in terms of the specific task, purpose, and audience; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and over-reliance on any one source and following a standard format for citation.

nodes.title: Analyze in detail how a complex primary source is structured, including how k...
nodes.description: Analyze in detail how a complex primary source is structured, including how key sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text contribute to the whole.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Analyze in detail how a complex primary source is structured, including how key sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text contribute to the whole.

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, includ...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including analyzing how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including analyzing how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).

nodes.title: Evaluate various explanations for actions or events and determine which expla...
nodes.description: Evaluate various explanations for actions or events and determine which explanation best accords with textual evidence, acknowledging where the text leaves matters uncertain.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Evaluate various explanations for actions or events and determine which explanation best accords with textual evidence, acknowledging where the text leaves matters uncertain.

nodes.title: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source
nodes.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary that makes clear the relationships among the key details and ideas.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary that makes clear the relationships among the key details and ideas.

nodes.title: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary s...
nodes.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, connecting insights gained from specific details to an understanding of the text as a whole.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, connecting insights gained from specific details to an understanding of the text as a whole.

nodes.title: By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades...
nodes.description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U
nodes.description: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (including The Declaration of Independence, the Preamble to the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address) for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze seventeenth-, eighteenth-, and nineteenth-century foundational U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (including The Declaration of Independence, the Preamble to the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, and Lincoln's Second Inaugural Address) for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.

nodes.title: Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U
nodes.description: Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning (e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court majority opinions and dissents) and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy (e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Delineate and evaluate the reasoning in seminal U.S. texts, including the application of constitutional principles and use of legal reasoning (e.g., in U.S. Supreme Court majority opinions and dissents) and the premises, purposes, and arguments in works of public advocacy (e.g., The Federalist, presidential addresses).

nodes.title: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different...
nodes.description: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words in order to address a question or solve a problem.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words in order to address a question or solve a problem.

nodes.title: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text in which the rhetori...
nodes.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how style and content contribute to the power, persuasiveness, or beauty of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how style and content contribute to the power, persuasiveness, or beauty of the text.

nodes.title: Analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his...
nodes.description: Analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his or her exposition or argument, including whether the structure makes points clear, convincing, and engaging.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze and evaluate the effectiveness of the structure an author uses in his or her exposition or argument, including whether the structure makes points clear, convincing, and engaging.

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, includ...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term or terms over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term or terms over the course of a text (e.g., how Madison defines faction in Federalist No. 10).

nodes.title: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific...
nodes.description: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific individuals, ideas, or events interact and develop over the course of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific individuals, ideas, or events interact and develop over the course of the text.

nodes.title: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development o...
nodes.description: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to provide a complex analysis; provide an objective summary of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to provide a complex analysis; provide an objective summary of the text.

nodes.title: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the tex...
nodes.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

nodes.title: By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dr...
nodes.description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century...
nodes.description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.

nodes.title: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e
nodes.description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)

nodes.title: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what i...
nodes.description: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).

nodes.title: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of...
nodes.description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, incl...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.)
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.)

nodes.title: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relat...
nodes.description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters are introduced and developed).

nodes.title: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their dev...
nodes.description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.

nodes.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.

nodes.description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to bu...
nodes.description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

nodes.title: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining...
nodes.description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
